what_it_does: This file exports the Desktop layout component, which arranges the application UI for a 1440×900 desktop viewport: a top chrome bar with control chips, an in-flow decisions bar, a main stage with left/right side strata and a centered wheel/instrument, plus a bottom scrubber and a tether indicator.
observations: The layout is wrapped in a div with class 'layout layout--desktop'
A top header ('bar bar--top') splits controls into a left group (LensChip, SpaceChip, LayerChip, ResChip, QuantChip, CentreChip) and a right group (LiveDot, ViewToggle, Settings)
DecisionsBar is rendered in-flow directly below the header with no props passed
The main stage uses a center section flanked by two asides: left strata shows Legend, right strata shows Disclosure when a point is selected and the gallery is closed, otherwise SelectHint
A Scrubber and Tether are rendered at the root level after main
Several elements carry stamped address attributes via stamp('ui://...') for instrumentation/addressing
standouts: Heavy use of small, single-purpose chip/toggle components imported from '../toggles' and '../decisions'
Right strata conditionally switches between a Disclosure detail panel and a selection hint
The component is purely presentational/layout: all state and callbacks arrive through the single 's' prop of type SurfaceState
concerns: DecisionsBar is invoked with no props, so its data source is not visible in this file
The 1440×900 breakpoint is only mentioned in a comment and not enforced here
No accessibility attributes (aria-label, role, etc.) are present on the landmark-like regions
notes: This is the desktop-specific shell that composes the app's chrome, side strata, and central wheel while keeping controls at the edges.
questions: How does DecisionsBar obtain its data without props?
Is the stamp() helper used for analytics, testing, or a runtime addressing system?
What does WheelOrState render when no wheel state is available?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the leading comment matches the implementation: centered instrument, ambient side strata, quiet corner chrome, no permanent control island.
apparent_themes: Declarative composition of many small UI units
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to create a calm, focused desktop workspace where the wheel/visualization dominates the field and controls are reachable but not central.
novelty: The use of stamp('ui://...') attributes to tag chrome and strata regions with stable addresses, and the placement of DecisionsBar as an in-flow strip below the header rather than inside the stage.
contribution: It contributes the desktop viewport layout composition, defining where each major control and content component lives and how the central instrument relates to side information panels.
summary_for_embedding: Desktop.tsx is a layout component that positions a top control bar, in-flow decisions strip, centered wheel, left/right side strata, scrubber, and tether for the desktop viewport of a surface application.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to provide a desktop-specific spatial arrangement that keeps the instrument central and controls peripheral.

symbols.does: Renders the desktop viewport layout shell composed of chrome bars, side strata, a central wheel, scrubber, and tether.
symbols.description: Desktop is an exported React component that receives the entire SurfaceState through a single 's' prop and arranges the application's desktop UI. It places toggle chips and settings in a top bar, renders a DecisionsBar below it, positions a Legend in the left strata and either a Disclosure panel or a selection hint in the right strata around a central WheelOrState, and finishes with a Scrubber and Tether. It also stamps stable ui:// addresses on key regions for instrumentation.
